intro: Bolt.new and Voxel51 overlap on scores and pricing posture, so the decision comes down to which problem each was actually built for. Bolt.new is browser-based full-stack AI app builder from StackBlitz with WebContainers. Voxel51 is open-source FiftyOne toolkit for computer-vision dataset and model evaluation. Pick Bolt.new when the job is apps and you accept low lock-in; pick Voxel51 when it is computer vision teams curating and debugging multimodal visual datasets and you accept low lock-in. The data we have shows Bolt.new at mostly transparent pricing and docs with a polished developer experience, and Voxel51 at mostly transparent pricing and docs with a polished developer experience. The honest trade-off: neither is universal — Bolt.new is a poor fit for simple projects, and Voxel51 is a poor fit for nlp-only teams or anyone without real cv workloads. Match the pricing model and lock-in level to how your team actually works, not the feature list.
quick_take: Bolt.new is for browser-based full-stack AI app builder from StackBlitz; Voxel51 is for open-source FiftyOne toolkit; decide based on ecosystem fit.
verdict_a: Choose Bolt.new if a free tier with paid upgrade from $0 is the right shape and the work maps to browser-based full-stack AI app builder from StackBlitz with WebContainers.
verdict_b: Choose Voxel51 if keeping the configuration portable matters and the work lines up with open-source FiftyOne toolkit for computer-vision dataset and model evaluation.

faq.a: We can't verify it cleanly: Bolt.new starts at $0 (freemium), while Voxel51 (freemium) doesn't publish an entry price. Request a quote from Voxel51 before assuming either side is cheaper.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Bolt.new to Voxel51?
faq.a: Migration in either direction is relatively cheap — both Bolt.new and Voxel51 are rated low lock-in, so your configuration and data should port without a rewrite. The realistic cost is team re-training and pipeline QA, not the tools themselves.

Both Bolt.new and Voxel51 rate the same on developer experience (5/5). The decision on DX then comes down to taste — which CLI, UI, or workflow matches your team's habits. A short side-by-side trial is the quickest way to tell.

faq.q: Is Voxel51 a good alternative to Bolt.new?
faq.a: Yes — Voxel51 is a reasonable alternative to Bolt.new for apps. The practical differences are freemium-vs-freemium billing and low-vs-low lock-in. If those fit your constraints better, treat Voxel51 as a credible swap.
